preview_text:
The model favours Iran at 48%, more than double Egypt's 22% win probability. This represents a substantial gap between the two teams in the statistical forecast, positioning Iran as clear favourite on a neutral ground.

The draw emerges as a genuine possibility in the model's distribution at 30%, narrowing the perceived gap between the teams somewhat. The win probabilities skew toward Iran, but the relatively high draw probability suggests the match carries competitive uncertainty rather than a heavily one-sided outlook.

The market diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both centre Iran at roughly 25%, roughly 23 percentage points below the model's 48%, while simultaneously pricing Egypt substantially higher—around 39%—than the model's 22%. This represents a material disagreement on the likely winner. The dashboard does not claim to know which framework better calibrates the outcome.
